title: 2 Terminology and Frequently Asked Questions
body_text:
2	Terminology and Frequently Asked Questions
2.1	Definitions
AAFC
Agriculture and Agri-Foods Canada (AAFC) provides information, research and technology, policies and programs to help Canada's agriculture, agri-food and agri-based products sector. It also competes in markets at home and abroad, manages risk, and embraces innovation.
Activity File
The Activity File is used to report the activity and balances in all AgriInvest Accounts held at a Financial Institution. This file is prepared at the end of each Reporting Period by the participating Financial Institution and submitted to Central 1.
Administration
Administration is the Federal or Provincial administration or agency that administers the AgriInvest program for a specific province.
AgriInvest Account
An AgriInvest Account is an account of deposits in the name of a Participant situated at a branch of the credit union and subject to the terms and conditions of the AgriInvest Program Credit Union Participation Agreement.
AgriInvest Account Initiation Form
The AgriInvest Account Initiation Form is issued by the Administration confirming the Participant芒聙聶s eligibility to participate in the AgriInvest Program and authorizing the Participant to open an AgriInvest Account.
AgriInvest Account Transfer Form
The AgriInvest Account Transfer Form is issued by the Administration to the AgriInvest Participants requesting a change to their AgriInvest Account from one Financial Institution to another. This form represents the Financial Institution芒聙聶s authority to open an AgriInvest Account on behalf of the Participant with the new institution.
AgriInvest Deposit Notice
The AgriInvest Deposit Notice is a notice sent to all Participants detailing their Allowable Net Sales maximum matchable deposit, and maximum account balance for the program year.
AgriInvest Officer
An AgriInvest Officer is an individual designated by the Financial Institution who is authorized to represent the Financial Institution with respect to AgriInvest Accounts and who will be the primary contact between Central 1 and the Financial Institution.
AgriInvest Program
The AgriInvest Program is a savings program for producers, which replaced the coverage under the CAIS Program for margin losses of 15% or less. Participant deposits and matching government contributions to AgriInvest Accounts provide producers with flexible coverage for small income declines as well as support for investments to mitigate risks or improve market income.
Allowable Net Sales (ANS)
Allowable Net Sales is the amount calculated by taking the sales of allowable agricultural commodities (including allowable program payments) less purchases of allowable agricultural commodities. A Participant's ANS (after adjustments) can芒聙聶t exceed $1 million for program purposes.
Client Centre
The Client Centre is a digital portal that provides secured access to Central 1芒聙聶s applications and to information and news about our products and services.
Financial Institution
The Financial Institution is a Canadian bank, credit union, caisse populaire, Credit Union Central, F脙漏d脙漏ration, or other institution that has signed an agreement with the Government of Canada to legally hold a participant's AgriInvest account. This also includes a credit union or caisse populaire which has entered into an agreement with the Credit Union Central or F脙漏d脙漏ration to be bound by the terms and conditions of the AgriInvest Financial Institution Master Agreement.
Letter of Direction
A Letter of Direction is a letter issued by the Administration for situations where other existing forms aren芒聙聶t applicable.
Master Agreement
The Master Agreement is the agreement between the Government of Canada and Central 1, providing for the holding of AgriInvest Participants芒聙聶 funds at Financial Institutions.
Matchable Deposit
A Matchable Deposit is an account deposit made by a Participant based on a percentage of ANS up to the established maximum.
Participant
A Participant is an agricultural producer who is eligible to participate in the AgriInvest Program and who has opened an AgriInvest Account with a Financial Institution.
PIN
A PIN is a government-issued Personal Identification Number that will appear on all forms issued by the Administration and which must be reported by Financial Institutions on the Activity File to assure prompt matching of information.
Reporting Period
The standard length of a Reporting Period is semi-monthly (i.e., 15th and month end) unless agreed otherwise between the Financial Institution and the Government of Canada. This also represents the time frame of transactions reported on the Activity File and the frequency of submission of the Activity File.
2.2	Frequently Asked Questions
AgriInvest Procedural Questions
Question 1
What file name should I use when submitting Activity Files to Central 1?
Answer
AgriInvest files must be named as
AG01MMDD.DAT
MM = month of the Reporting Period
DD = start day of the Reporting Period
Reporting Periods run from the
芒聙聯 1st to 15th of the month
芒聙聯 16th to end of month
The day of the reporting period start date is always either 01 or 16.
Question 2
How do I report account activity if accounts reside in more than one branch (i.e., transit number)?
Answer
Central 1芒聙聶s file format can only handle one A & Z record. Input the main Head Office transit number in the A record and input the transactions from the multiple branches under it. Each transaction identifies the branch.
Question 3
How do I create test files without impacting the production environment?
Answer
Financial Institutions can send a request to
BusinessDevelopmentandDelivery@central1.com
to make arrangements for sending a test file. Use 芒聙聹9999芒聙聺 as the Institution number in the A Record, and upload the Activity File to Central 1. The AgriInvest system will process the file and generate the corresponding confirmation emails, but it won芒聙聶t forward the data to AAFC.
Question 4
What happens if I don芒聙聶t receive an email notification from Central 1 after I芒聙聶ve submitted an Activity File?
Answer
The AgriInvest system didn芒聙聶t recognize the file format. The system only recognizes files in CSV format. See Question 1.
Question 5
I芒聙聶ve received a file rejection email from Central 1. What do I do?
Answer
A file will be rejected if the Reporting Period date is incorrect in the file name. The rejection may also be based on schema errors within the file (e.g., incorrect date format, duplicate account numbers, etc.).
Resolve the issues reported in the email from Central 1 and submit a new Activity File before the reporting deadline.
Question 6
What are the reporting cut-off times?
Answer
File pickup times are scheduled every half hour between 6 am PT and 6 pm PT on the first three business days after each reporting period.
Central 1 recommends that Financial Institutions submit files within the first two business days. Ideally, the third day is used for file cleanup.
The AAFC report is submitted at the end of the third business day following the final pickup.
Question 7
What happens if a Financial Institution misses the cut-off?
Answer
Reminder emails are sent to avoid missing deadlines. Central 1 will call the Financial Institution if no response is received. In an emergency situation, Central 1 will make alternate arrangements.
The AgriInvest Activity Files are amalgamated into regional files:
- BC
- ON
- Manitoba
- Atlantic
A regional file can芒聙聶t be transmitted to AAFC until the Activity Files from all the Financial Institutions within that region have been received by Central 1. If any Activity Files are missing from a regional file, that entire region will be rejected by AAFC.
Question 8
What is a PIN?
Answer
A PIN or Participant Identification Number is the number issued by AAFC to identify individual producers. The PIN must be reported on the Activity File.
Question 9
Can AgriInvest deposits be used for security purposes?
Answer
AAFC regulations don芒聙聶t permit the use of AgriInvest deposits for security purposes, and the money in AgriInvest Accounts isn芒聙聶t subject to attachment, seizure, and execution except in accordance with the
Farm Income Protection Act
.
Question 10
Do I need to report accounts even though they had no activity in the last Reporting Period?
Answer
All accounts need to be reported in every Reporting Period even if there was no change in activity. This will provide the AgriInvest Program administrators confirmation of all active accounts and ensure correct opening balances are reported based on the previously reported closing balance.
Question 11
I need/want to change the account numbers reported for producers. Can I just enter the new account numbers in the next report?
Answer
You
can芒聙聶t
change an account number once it has been reported. The old account must be closed in one Reporting Period and be re-opened as a new account number in the next Reporting Period. When the new account is reopened the opening balance must show zero and a deposit must be shown for the balance transfer from the old account.
Question 12
Does the opening balance on an account have to match the closing account balance from the previous report?
Answer
Yes. The opening balance for an account in the current file must equal the closing balance of that account from the previous file.
END
